description:
You are an operations engineer who has just finished triaging a mock production
    incident.  Your objectives are to document the incident in Markdown, then run a
    very small “linter” that confirms every line in the document is ≤ 80 characters
    long.

    1.  Inside the directory /home/user/incidents/ there is already a file named
        INCIDENT_TEMPLATE.md that contains section headings and placeholder text.
        Replace the placeholders with concrete details and save the filled-in
        document under the exact name

            /home/user/incidents/INC0001_2024-02-15.md

        The new file MUST contain **exactly** the text shown below, no extra blank
        lines, no trailing spaces, and all lines must be ≤ 80 characters long.  The
        automated checker will compare your file byte-for-byte.

        --------------------- START OF REQUIRED FILE CONTENT ---------------------
        Incident ID: INC0001
        Date: 2024-02-15

        Summary
        -------
        During routine load testing the API returned 500 errors for 35 % of requests.

        Root Cause
        ----------
        The file descriptor limit on api-vm-3 was reached.
        This caused nginx to refuse new connections.

        Impact
        ------
        HTTP traffic to the public API was partially degraded for 7 minutes.

        Mitigation
        ----------
        The ulimit for open files was raised from 1024 to 8192 and nginx was reloaded.

        Postmortem Actions
        ------------------
        1. Add file-descriptor-usage alert to Prometheus.
        2. Document the new ulimit value in the deployment playbook.
        3. Schedule a capacity review for all API nodes.
        ---------------------- END OF REQUIRED FILE CONTENT ----------------------

    2.  After the Markdown file is saved, run a simple “lint” pass that checks
        for lines longer than 80 characters.  Write the linter’s single-line
        outcome to

            /home/user/incidents/lint_report.log

        The linter output must be **exactly**

            No issues found

        followed by a single newline character.  If you script this with awk,
        sed, or any other standard GNU/Linux tool, that is fine; just be sure the
        resulting log file matches the required content precisely.

    Summary of artefacts that will be graded:

      • /home/user/incidents/INC0001_2024-02-15.md (must match the required text
        above byte-for-byte)
      • /home/user/incidents/lint_report.log (must contain the exact string
        “No issues found\n”)

    Use any sequence of terminal commands you like (it should only take a couple
    of commands: one to create the Markdown file and one to generate the log).
    When both files are present and correct, the task is complete.
truth:
Expected file 1: /home/user/incidents/INC0001_2024-02-15.md

    Exact contents (including final newline):
Incident ID: INC0001
Date: 2024-02-15

Summary
-------
During routine load testing the API returned 500 errors for 35 % of requests.

Root Cause
----------
The file descriptor limit on api-vm-3 was reached.
This caused nginx to refuse new connections.

Impact
------
HTTP traffic to the public API was partially degraded for 7 minutes.

Mitigation
----------
The ulimit for open files was raised from 1024 to 8192 and nginx was reloaded.

Postmortem Actions
------------------
1. Add file-descriptor-usage alert to Prometheus.
2. Document the new ulimit value in the deployment playbook.
3. Schedule a capacity review for all API nodes.

    Expected file 2: /home/user/incidents/lint_report.log

    Exact contents (including final newline):
No issues found
name: task_000000_e7a962de
